plan: Trim SPY to reduce concentration and bring it within limits.
Sell all holdings flagged by the exit-scanner (QQQI, QQQ, SMH) and all leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) to preserve capital.
Reduce CRWD to manage single-name concentration.
Allocate a portion to XLF, a high-scoring financial sector setup, for diversification and quality.
Maintain a significant cash buffer of 15-20% by reducing overall exposure.
This plan focuses on capital preservation and reduced drawdown.
conservative.read: This conservative approach targets around 1.5% per month, prioritizing capital preservation. Expect lower volatility and drawdown, but also reduced upside potential. Risk is significantly lowered by exiting leveraged and downtrending positions.

moves.note: Reduce leveraged ETF to small size
Reduce leveraged ETF to small size

plan: Trim SPY to meet the concentration mandate of 25%.
Sell QQQI, QQQ, and SMH, which are flagged by the exit-scanner.
Reduce SOXL to a small, manageable size and exit SPAL.
Initiate new positions in XOM and AMD, both high-scoring scanner setups, to diversify and add quality growth.
Maintain a cash buffer of approximately 10% to manage risk and provide flexibility.
This plan balances risk and growth, aiming for a more diversified portfolio.
balanced.read: This balanced approach targets around 2.5% per month, focusing on risk-first growth. It aims for a blend of broad index exposure, quality tech, and some diversification. Expect moderate volatility and drawdown, with reasonable growth potential.

plan: Trim SPY to meet the 25% concentration mandate.
Sell QQQI, QQQ, and SMH, which are flagged by the exit-scanner.
Increase exposure to SOXL, a leveraged ETF, leveraging its high scanner score.
Initiate new positions in AMD and META, both high-scoring tech setups, to drive growth.
Maintain a smaller cash buffer of approximately 5% to maximize market exposure.
This plan aggressively leans into high-conviction setups and leverage.
aggressive.read: This aggressive approach targets around 4% per month, aiming for stretch growth. It involves higher leverage and concentration in high-scoring setups, which amplifies both upside and drawdown. Be aware of daily decay in leveraged ETFs and the potential for significant losses if strict stop discipline is not maintained.

plan: Trim SPY to meet the 25% concentration mandate.
Increase QQQI, an income-focused ETF, to prioritize monthly distributions.
Sell QQQ, CRWD, SMH, SOXL, and SPAL to reduce high-beta exposure and exit leveraged ETFs.
Initiate a position in XLF, a high-scoring financial sector setup, for diversification and stability.
Maintain a significant cash buffer to ensure liquidity and manage market volatility.
This plan prioritizes steady monthly distributions and low drawdown.
income.read: This income approach targets around 1% per month, focusing on parking cash for yield. It prioritizes low drawdown and reliable income over growth, rotating into income-generating ETFs. Be aware that distributions are variable and NAV can still fall in a selloff; this is not a savings account.
